title: Crepidium trukense
intro: Crepidium trukense, also known as Truk orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Pacific island of Chuuk. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium trukense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepidium trukense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Crepidium trukense is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the island of Chuuk in Micronesia. This plant is a part of the genus Crepidium, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The classification of Crepidium trukense is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Liliopsida. Within the family Orchidaceae, it belongs to the subfamily Vanilloideae.
